# Overview

The talk is about the shift from typing code to steering agents in public-sector engineering.

The concrete case study is Challenge 2 from the AI Engineering Lab Codex build: government guidance exists, but it is scattered across PDFs, Word files, spreadsheets, and shared-drive style material. The engineering problem is not just whether AI can answer a question; it is whether a team can build source-backed, inspectable answers with provenance and validation.

The narrative pack keeps the imported visual material close to the evidence. Each slide, PDF page, and standalone image now has a sidecar note that describes the visible point, records tags and caveats, and links back into the research pack or repository artefacts.

Use the pack as a browseable companion during or after the talk rather than as a replacement for the live demo.
